mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises above the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52% over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases consistently precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns during early-cycle expansions, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, directly translating to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY growth surpasses 4.0%, invalidating the inflation-aversion mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold strategy over 20-day horizons, with cumulative alpha below 0.05% per month in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production stagnates while aggregate demand remains elevated, supply bottlenecks emerge, raising input costs and compressing corporate margins, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth lags GDPC1 growth by > 1.0%
failure_condition: Periods of INDPRO stagnation relative to GDPC1 consistently coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns, or the signal hit rate falls below 45% in walk-forward validation.
